page: Amazon Web Services m5d.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor architecture. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 dedicated virtual CPUs, which correspond to 8 physical cores utilizing hyper-threading. System memory consists of 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with the instance, providing a 600 GB NVMe SSD instance store, while network connectivity is established at a baseline speed of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, measured across stress-ng and Geekbench tests, falls into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core execution, including multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests, reaches average middle-tier performance. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached operations remains in the average range, with uncached read speeds at 89.01 GB/sec and write speeds at 47.12 GB/sec.

The instance exhibits average performance for database operations, Redis SET commands, and static web serving. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference using a small 135M model achieves average speeds, whereas prompt processing on a medium 7B model drops to the bottom 25% tier. Given the presence of local NVMe storage and balanced memory-to-vCPU allocation, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, caching layers, and database applications requiring temporary local storage, though it presents a performance tradeoff for single-threaded or compute-heavy tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It includes a bundled 600 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks indicate a tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the bottom 25% tier, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database transactions perform in the average middle 50% range. Lacking a GPU, the instance is not designed for heavy acceleration but supports standard workloads. The combination of balanced memory density and local NVMe storage makes this instance suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database workloads that benefit from high-speed temporary local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.4xlarge is an x86_64 instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 600 GB NVMe SSD, suited for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring balanced memory, dedicated Intel Xeon cores, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for standard workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB DDR4 RAM
600 GB local NVMe SSD instance store for low-latency temporary storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Tradeoff of weaker single-core and single-threaded processing speeds
Suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database operations
